title: Kontiki, an independent gay magazine, launches in Switzerland
description: In May 1978 the first issue of Kontiki appeared, the first Swiss gay magazine published independently of any activist group.

title: Switzerland's first Christopher Street Day takes place in Zurich
description: On 24 June 1978 Zurich hosted the first Christopher Street Day demonstration held in Switzerland, opening an annual tradition of national pride marches.

title: Swiss gay working groups expand into a trilingual national coordination
description: On 8-9 July 1978 the Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Schweiz (HACH) admitted the Geneva group GHOG and the Lausanne group GLH, adopting the French name Coordination Homosexuelle Suisse (CHOSE); an Italian designation, Coordinamento Omosessuale Svizzera (COSA), followed later.

title: International Lesbian and Gay Association founded in Coventry
description: Delegates meeting in Coventry, England on 30 September 1978 created the International Gay Association (IGA), the worldwide federation of LGBTQ organizations later renamed ILGA.

title: Geneva hosts Switzerland's first film festival on homosexuality
description: From 31 October to 19 November 1978 Geneva staged "Cinema et Homosexualite", the first Swiss film festival devoted to homosexual themes.

title: Zurich abolishes its police register of homosexuals
description: On 1 February 1979 the Zurich police register of homosexual men, kept for decades of surveillance, was officially abolished, with its records destroyed.

title: Gay working group HALU forms in Lucerne
description: The Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Luzern (HALU) held its founding on 12 May 1979, extending the Swiss activist network into central Switzerland.

title: Second Swiss Christopher Street Day held in Bern
description: Switzerland's second Christopher Street Day took place on 23 June 1979, moving the national pride demonstration from Zurich to the federal capital Bern.

title: Mixed gay activist group Arcade forms in Basel-Land
description: In 1979 activists in the canton of Basel-Land created Arcade, a homosexual working group open to both women and men.

title: Gay working group HAA forms in Aargau
description: The Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Aargau (HAA) formed in 1979, bringing organized gay activism to the canton of Aargau.

title: GAY 80 national gay and lesbian day held in Basel
description: On 21 June 1980 Basel hosted GAY 80, a national day for gay men and lesbians that served as Switzerland's third Christopher Street Day.

title: Homodemo 81 draws around 1,000 marchers in Lausanne
description: Switzerland's fourth Christopher Street Day, staged as Homodemo 81 in Lausanne on 4 July 1981, brought roughly 1,000 participants onto the streets.

title: Working group on homosexuality within the churches forms in Bern
description: On 25 April 1982 the HuK group, dedicated to homosexuality within the churches, held its founding in Bern, linking Swiss gay activism with religious communities.

title: SOH launches SPOT25 youth group
description: In 1982 the Swiss homophile organization SOH set up its gay youth group under the name SPOT25, giving young gay men their own structure.

title: Fifth Swiss Christopher Street Day returns to Zurich
description: On 26 June 1982 Zurich hosted the fifth Swiss Christopher Street Day, staged under a title playfully evoking the traditional Swiss open-air assembly.

title: HAB starts a group for pupils and apprentices in Bern
description: In 1982 the Bern gay working group HAB created a dedicated group for school pupils and apprentices, reaching out to its youngest members.

title: Dialogai founded in Geneva
description: In November 1982 Geneva activists established Dialogai, which grew into the leading gay organization of French-speaking Switzerland.

title: First AIDS cases recorded in Switzerland
description: In 1982 Switzerland registered its first AIDS patients, marking the arrival of the epidemic that would reshape the country's gay community over the following decade.

title: Swiss homosexual teachers organize as VHELS
description: On 6 March 1983 homosexual educators across Switzerland formed VHELS (also known by its French acronym OSEEH), a national association for gay teachers.

title: HAZ opens the Centro community space in Zurich
description: In May 1983 the Zurich gay working group HAZ moved into premises on Sihlquai, opening its community center known as the Centro.

title: Sixth Swiss Christopher Street Day held in Lucerne
description: Lucerne hosted the sixth Swiss Christopher Street Day on 25 June 1983, bringing the national pride march to central Switzerland.

title: Homosexual organizations HOT form in Thurgau
description: In September 1983 activists in the canton of Thurgau established the Homosexuelle Organisationen Thurgau (HOT).

title: Gay group PRO forms in Ticino
description: In 1983 the group Promozione Relazioni Omo-sociali (PRO) formed in Ticino, organizing gay life in Italian-speaking Switzerland.

title: Gay health professionals organize under the HACH umbrella
description: On 11 February 1984 gay men working in medicine formed their own group, known as the Medizinmaenner, under the umbrella of the national coordination HACH.

title: Seventh Swiss Christopher Street Day held in Bern
description: Bern hosted the seventh Swiss Christopher Street Day on 23 June 1984, continuing the annual national pride demonstration.

title: Gay Pride march held in Basel
description: On 30 June 1984, one week after the national Christopher Street Day in Bern, Basel held its own Gay Pride march.

title: Zurich University Hospital holds its first AIDS information evening
description: On 13 December 1984 the University Hospital in Zurich hosted its first public information evening on AIDS, an early step in Swiss public education on the epidemic.

title: Gay working group HASO forms in Solothurn
description: In 1984 activists in the canton of Solothurn created the Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Solothurn (HASO).

title: HAZ forms an AIDS group in Zurich
description: On 5 January 1985 the Zurich gay working group HAZ set up its own AIDS group, an early community response to the epidemic in Switzerland.

title: Aids-Hilfe Schweiz founded as national AIDS organization
description: On 2 June 1985 the Aids-Hilfe Schweiz (AHS) was established as the national Swiss AIDS support organization, coordinating prevention work with regional branches across the country.

title: Eighth Swiss Christopher Street Day marches in Basel
description: On 15 June 1985 Basel hosted the eighth Swiss Christopher Street Day under the motto "Recht auf Lieben" (the right to love).

title: Gay magazines Kontiki and anderschume merge
description: In 1985 the Swiss gay periodicals Kontiki and anderschume combined into anderschume/Kontiki, a merged magazine appearing on a regular schedule.

title: Andre Ratti publicly discloses his AIDS diagnosis
description: At a press conference in Bern on 2 July 1985, television presenter Andre Ratti, a co-founder of Aids-Hilfe Schweiz, went before the media with his AIDS diagnosis — the first prominent Swiss figure to do so.

title: Zurich AIDS support organization ZAH founded
description: On 5 November 1985 the Zuercher Aids-Hilfe (ZAH) was established as the regional AIDS support organization for Zurich.

title: Bern AIDS support organization AHB founded
description: On 1 December 1985 the Aids-Hilfe Bern (AHB) was established, adding a Bern branch to Switzerland's growing network of regional AIDS organizations.

title: HAZ opens the Schwubliothek gay library in Zurich
description: In 1985 the Zurich gay working group HAZ opened the Schwubliothek, its library with a lending service for gay literature.

title: AIDS organizations form in Ticino and eastern Switzerland
description: In 1985 Aiuto-Aids Ticino began work in Italian-speaking Switzerland, while the AHSGA opened as the AIDS advice service for St. Gallen and the two Appenzell cantons.

title: Dialogai sets up an AIDS working group in Geneva
description: In 1985 the Geneva gay organization Dialogai created its own working group on AIDS, bringing prevention work to French-speaking Switzerland.

title: AIDS support organization founded for central Switzerland
description: In March 1986 the Aids-Hilfe Luzern opened, covering the cantons of Lucerne, Obwalden, Nidwalden and Uri.

title: AIDS information brochure delivered to every Swiss household
description: In March 1986 a detailed AIDS information brochure was distributed to every household in Switzerland, one of the earliest nationwide mailings on the epidemic.

title: Ninth Swiss Christopher Street Day joins Zurich's 2000-year celebration
description: From 10 to 14 June 1986 Zurich hosted the ninth Swiss Christopher Street Day; with the city marking its 2000-year anniversary, the pride event ran under the motto "zuerischwul2000".

title: Andre Ratti dies of AIDS
description: Television presenter Andre Ratti, the first prominent Swiss figure to disclose his AIDS diagnosis publicly, died on 25 October 1986.

title: Gay community magazine Cruiser publishes its pilot issue
description: On 12 December 1986 the pilot issue of Cruiser appeared, a Swiss magazine for the gay community that became a long-running title.

title: Regional AIDS organizations form in Basel and Thurgau/Schaffhausen
description: In 1986 two further regional AIDS organizations opened in Switzerland: the Aids-Hilfe beider Basel (AHbB) and the Aids-Hilfe Thurgau/Schaffhausen (AHTS).

title: Gay Bikers Zurich motorcycle club forms
description: In 1986 gay motorcyclists in Zurich formed the Gay Bikers Zuerich (GBZ), adding a sports-and-leisure club to the city's gay scene.

title: Switzerland launches the national Stop Aids prevention campaign
description: On 3 February 1987 the national AIDS prevention campaign "Stop Aids" started, becoming one of the most visible public-health campaigns in Swiss history.

title: Youth group Spot25 starts its own bulletin
description: In August 1987 the Swiss gay youth group Spot25 began publishing spot-news, a bulletin appearing every two months.

title: 600,000 march for lesbian and gay rights in Washington
description: On 11 October 1987 some 600,000 people marched in Washington, D.C. for lesbian and gay rights; the date's anniversary was later established as National Coming Out Day.

title: National gay demonstration held in Bern instead of a CSD
description: On 17 October 1987 no Christopher Street Day took place in Switzerland; a national gay demonstration in Bern was staged in its place.

title: ILGA holds its world conference in Zurich
description: From 28 to 30 December 1987 the ILGA world conference met in Zurich, organized jointly by the Swiss groups SOH, HACH and HAZ.
